mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict returns in the top quartile of unemployment shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in consumer credit and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate cash flow expectations, depressing equity valuations over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample prediction, or forward returns do not diverge significantly when both proxies decline simultaneously versus when only one declines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects pent-up demand fulfillment and capacity utilization normalization, which supports corporate margins and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with forward equity returns during recovery periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when adjusted for capacity utilization metrics.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 305 indicate entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL breaches the 305 threshold, or the threshold signal exhibits no statistical edge over a flat baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive index returns over a quarterly horizon regardless of cyclical phase.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows zero correlation with forward equity returns over quarterly windows, or earnings per share growth decouples from GDP growth for more than two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls growth and a rising unemployment rate signals a structural labor market mismatch that increases wage inflation pressures, squeezing corporate profit margins and depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict margin compression or equity underperformance, or forward returns do not systematically decline when payrolls rise while unemployment simultaneously increases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader economic activity and generates negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts breach the 1400 threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a flat baseline in walk-forward testing.
